content: My understanding is that bindgen does not translate macros, therefore if we wanted to use something like virt_mb() or barrier() is there somewhere those are available? If they aren't where should I place them? Rust supports in-line assembly so I could just have my own macro, but I think something like virt_mb() would be something others would also want.
Take a look at helpers.c -- we re-expose macros there as regular functions.
The problem with exporting a macro like virt_mb or barrier to regular functions will be too heavy weight. We don't want to pay for the extra function call overhead.
Cross-language LTO should mostly take care of that.
But yeah, eventually, it is likely we may end up with some duplicate functionality to improve that.
However, for the moment, I think avoiding duplicate implementations on both sides is best.

Hello everyone,
I have been playing with the rust support in Linux but I am having trouble using Macros. I am always getting a linking error.
Minimal reproduction scenario:

diff --git a/rust/kernel/miscdev.rs b/rust/kernel/miscdev.rs
index ccbd4f4cfe28..a460935523cc 100644
--- a/rust/kernel/miscdev.rs
+++ b/rust/kernel/miscdev.rs
@@ -7,6 +7,7 @@
 //! Reference: <https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/latest/driver-api/misc_devices.html>

 use crate::bindings;
+use crate::bindings::BUG;
 use crate::error::{Error, Result};
 use crate::file_operations::{FileOpenAdapter, FileOperations, FileOperationsVtable};
 use crate::{str::CStr, KernelModule, ThisModule};
@@ -73,6 +74,10 @@ impl<T: FileOperations> Registration<T> {
             return Err(Error::EINVAL);
         }

+        let _ = unsafe {
+            BUG();
+        };
+
         // SAFETY: The adapter is compatible with `misc_register`.
         this.mdev.fops = unsafe { FileOperationsVtable::<Self, T>::build() };
         this.mdev.name = name.as_char_ptr();

Error:

  OBJCOPY arch/x86/boot/compressed/vmlinux.bin
ERROR: modpost: "rust_helper_BUG" [samples/rust/rust_random.ko] undefined!
ERROR: modpost: "rust_helper_BUG" [samples/rust/rust_semaphore.ko] undefined!
ERROR: modpost: "rust_helper_BUG" [samples/rust/rust_miscdev.ko] undefined!
make[1]: *** [scripts/Makefile.modpost:134: modules-only.symvers] Error 1

Should I enable some setting or am I missing something? @Miguel Ojeda
That function is not exported. You need to add EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(rust_helper_BUG); to rust/helpers.c
Ahh! Of course, I don't know how I missed that! Thanks @Wei Liu
Yeah. @Pankaj Raghav please note BUG is not a macro in the Rust side (and using C macros in Rust code is not possible in general).
I found a few more and thought I'd open a PR https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/637 .

Thanks @Miguel Ojeda!
Yeah, I understood that. I actually was trying something with PCI and trying to expose a macro. I forgot to add EXPORT_SYMBOL. I used BUG here as an example. :)
